## [00:00 - 02:08]

This is the podcast shot. I'm John Loomer. Today we're gonna talk about whether you should try to be on every platform. Let's go. Should you be everywhere? Maybe. I hear advice that you shouldn't try to be on every platform. You should only focus on one or two. I agreed with this for years and I focused primarily on Facebook. But my outlook has changed with short form video. The beauty of short form video is that the format is mostly the same across TikTok, Reels, YouTube Shorts, and elsewhere. So I published the exact same video across as many as eight different platforms and many people would disagree with that approach. But your followers are often going to be different across platforms. But meanwhile your target audience could still remain the same. I don't feel like I need to change my content for the platform. It might be ideal to, but you don't have to. And I'm seeing the impact of that. I'm reaching people that I wouldn't have otherwise. In just over six months I've built new audiences and I've attracted new business across multiple platforms. All while sharing the exact same content to each. So yeah, you can be everywhere and you should try to be if you can. You won't necessarily dedicate the same amount of time to connecting on each platform, of course, still prioritize. But experiment, see what happens. You might be surprised. Last call! I hope you enjoyed this episode of the Pubcast with John Loomer. This was a shot, which is a shorter episode. Sometimes shots take from short form videos I create on TikTok, Reels, YouTube Shorts, and elsewhere. And sometimes like here, I record a shot just for the pubcast. Of course, we also have longer episodes I try to publish once or twice per month. So if you enjoyed this, don't forget to subscribe, rate, review, and drop me a message. Let me know what challenges you're facing at John at JohnLoomer.com. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
